item.title: Why federal agencies need a mission-centered cyber response | IBM
item.description: In today's cyber landscape, government agencies cannot treat cybersecurity issues as a problem for IT departments to tackle alone.

item.title: What the Data Is Telling Us About the Current Rise in Security Threats During the COVID-19 Pandemic | IBM
item.description: IBM X-Force Research has been seeing a significant number of new malicious domains related to COVID-19 appear in the wild since late February 2020, based on Quad 9 data.

item.title: Trickbot Group's AnchorDNS Backdoor Upgrades to AnchorMail | IBM
item.description: Researchers from IBM Security X-Force have discovered a new version of Trickbot's AnchorDNS backdoor being used in recent ransomware attacks. Get the in-depth analysis here.

item.title: Vulnerable Powerline Extenders Underline Lax Security for the Internet of Things | IBM
item.description: Multiple vulnerabilities found in Tenda PA6 Wi-Fi Powerline extender, version 1.0.1.21, could potentially allow a remote attacker to gain complete control over a device.

item.title: When the Absence of Noise Becomes Signal: Defensive Considerations for Lazarus FudModule | IBM
item.description: Explore opportunities for detection of the FudModule within a Lazarus malware sample analyzed by IBM Security X-Force.

item.title: Unprecedented Shift: The Trickbot Group is Systematically Attacking Ukraine | IBM
item.description: IBM Security X-Force uncovered evidence indicating that the Russia-based cybercriminal syndicate "Trickbot group" has been attacking Ukraine since the Russian invasion. Explore an in-depth analysis on six of ITG23's campaigns.

item.title: What’s New in the 2020 Cost of a Data Breach Report | IBM
item.description: The global total cost of a data breach averaged $3.86 million in this year’s study. See five key findings from the 2020 Cost of a Data Breach Report

item.title: What Drives Incident Responders: Key Findings from the 2022 Incident Responder Study | IBM
item.description: Unpack the experiences of a modern-day cybersecurity incident responder in IBM Security's 2022 Incident Responder Study.

item.title: Video Conferencing Security for Businesses Isn't a New Issue, It Just Came Into Focus | IBM
item.description: Video conferencing has suddenly become a critical tool for businesses. Here are some tips for increased privacy and control.

item.title: What’s new in the 2021 Cost of a Data Breach Report - Security Intelligence | IBM
item.description: In 2021, the average cost of a data breach reached a record high of $4.24 million. Get the latest findings on zero trust, AI, cloud security and more from the 2021 Cost of a Data Breach Report.

item.title: When a Cyber Crisis Hits, Know Your OODA Loops | IBM
item.description: OODA loops can help System 1-thinking security professionals react swiftly to cyberattacks and embrace an intuitive approach to incident response.

item.title: Why Phishing Is Still the Top Attack Method | IBM
item.description: Phishing attackers manipulate people's expectations to bait their hooks. See how to protect your employees from today's top attack type.

item.title: Understanding the Adversary: How Ransomware Attacks Happen | IBM
item.description: Learn the five stages of ransomware attacks — and how to defend against them — based on analysis of hundreds of ransomware incidents from IBM Security X-Force.

item.title: Hunting for Evidence of DLL Side-Loading With PowerShell and Sysmon| IBM
item.description: X-Force Red released a tool called Windows Feature Hunter, which identifies targets for DLL Side-Loading on a Windows system using Frida. See how it works.

item.title: X-Force Red Launches in Latin America and Takes on the Evolution of Cybersecurity | IBM
item.description: IBM's X-Force Red team delivers the offensive security capabilities organizations need to protect against and respond to advanced targeted attacks.

item.title: Don’t Be Rude, Stay: Avoiding Fork&Run .NET Execution With InlineExecute-Assembly | IBM
item.description: Get an in-depth look at the new BOF that allows operators to execute. NET assemblies in process via Cobalt Strike. Learn more from X-Force Red's offensive security experts.

item.title: IBM X-Force Research: What You Need to Know About Injection Attacks - Security Intelligence | IBM
item.description: Read this IBM X-Force Research Report to learn how attackers are using injection attacks and what steps you can take to help protect your systems and data.

item.title: How One Application Test Uncovered an Unexpected Opening in an Enterprise Call Tool | IBM
item.description: Our application penetration test against the CUCM revealed more than we expected. See how we discovered this problem and how to protect your business.

item.title: The Sodinokibi Chronicles: A (R)Evil Cybercrime Gang Disrupting Organizations for Trade Secrets and Cash | IBM
item.description: Sodinokibi/REvil emerged in 2019 and has gained considerable momentum. See the indicators of compromise and recent evolutions of this attack.

item.title: Zero Trust and DNS Security: Better Together | IBM
item.description: Discover how a zero trust framework and DNS security work hand in hand.

item.title: X-Force uncovers global NetScaler Gateway credential harvesting campaign | IBM
item.description: IBM X-Force uncovered a campaign where attackers were exploiting the vulnerability identified in CVE-2023-3519 to attack NetScaler Gateways to insert a malicious script into the HTML content of the authentication web page to capture user credentials.

item.title: X-Force Prevents Zero Day from Going Anywhere | IBM
item.description: Dive into the investigation of a zero-day attack that took advantage of a vulnerability in Forta’s file-transfer tool, GoAnyWhere. Explore the research from IBM X-Force experts.

item.title: X-Force Hive0129 targeting financial institutions in LATAM with a custom banking trojan | IBM
item.description: Read the in-depth analysis on BlotchyQuasar, a banking trojan hardcoded to collect credentials from Latin American-based banking applications and websites. More from IBM Security X-Force.

item.title: Before, During and After an Attack - Security Intelligence | IBM
item.description: Learn how IBM X-Force Incident Response and Intelligence Services can help you build a holistic program to fight the security challenges your organization faces.

item.title: IBM X-Force Research: Security Trends in the Manufacturing Industry - Security Intelligence | IBM
item.description: This IBM X-Force research report examines the latest security trends in the manufacturing industry, which was the third most attacked sector in 2016.

item.title: 2016: The Year Menaces Multiplied - Security Intelligence | IBM
item.description: Preview the key findings from the 2017 IBM X-Force Threat Intelligence Index in this infographic and then download the complete report to learn more.

item.title: Hunting for Windows “Features” with Frida: DLL Sideloading | IBM
item.description: X-Force Red experts take a deep dive into DLL sideloading and how offensive security professionals can prevent attackers from using it.

item.title: X-Force Identifies Vulnerability in IoT Platform | IBM
item.description: Take a look at a new vulnerability discovered in the IoT platform, ThingsBoard. More from IBM Security X-Force experts.

item.title: X-Force data reveals top spam trends, campaigns and senior superlatives in 2023 | IBM
item.description: The 2024 IBM X-Force Threat Intelligence Index revealed attackers continued to pivot to evade detection to deliver their malware in 2023. Learn more about the top trends and attack campaigns of 2023.

item.title: IBM X-Force Research: Cybercrime Riding Tax Season Tides - Security Intelligence
item.description: This IBM X-Force report illustrates how cybercriminals are using the dark web to coordinate tax fraud attacks.

item.title: IBM Report Finds Health Care Data at Growing Risk From Ransomware, Insiders and Third-Party Breaches
item.description: A new report based on IBM MSS data revealed that ransomware, insider threats and third-party breaches plagued health care organizations in 2016.

item.title: X-Force releases detection & response framework for managed file transfer software | IBM
item.description: Mass exploitation of managed file transfer (MFT) software is on the rise. To address this, IBM Security X-Force has released a framework for detection and response for MFT attacks.

item.title: IBM identifies zero-day vulnerability in Zyxel NAS devices | IBM
item.description: IBM X-Force uncovered two new flaws when investigating a vulnerability affecting Zyxel NAS devices. Learn more about the risks and the patch put in place.

item.title: Not-So-Terrible Twos: IBM X-Force Exchange Celebrates Its Second Birthday
item.description: Since its inception two years ago, the IBM X-Force Exchange has grown into a comprehensive hub for industrywide threat intelligence collaboration.

item.title: X-Force Research Update: Top 10 Cybersecurity Vulnerabilities of 2021 | IBM
item.description: IBM X-Force is at the forefront of cybersecurity, providing solutions and support against various cyber threats. See what the team lists as the top 10 cybersecurity vulnerabilities of 2021.

item.title: X-Force Report: No Shortage of Resources Aimed at Hacking Cloud Environments | IBM
item.description: From human error to a burgeoning dark web marketplace, see how cybercriminals are evolving their attacks on cloud environments. Get the latest from the 2021 X-Force Cloud Threat Landscape Report.

item.title: One Big Lesson From the Cyber Range to Help Solve Confirmation Bias | IBM
item.description: The IBM X-Force IRIS team practices a method called dual verification in the cyber range to help overcome confirmation bias and arrive at ground truth more quickly.

item.title: Mobile Banking Trojans as Keen on Cryptocurrency as PC Malware | IBM
item.description: Cybercriminals are emulating PC malware to develop mobile banking Trojans that leverage malicious miners to steal cryptocurrency and deliver it to attacker-controlled wallets.

item.title: What Hurricane Preparedness Can Teach Us About Ransomware | IBM
item.description: Explore the parallels in preparing for a natural disaster and preparing for a cyber crisis.

item.title: X-Force Threat Intelligence Index 2024 reveals stolen credentials as top risk, with AI attacks on the horizon | IBM
item.description: Explore top findings from the 2024 X-Force Threat Intelligence Index, and learn how your organization can defend against evolving threats.

item.title: Your BOFs Are gross, Put on a Mask: How to Hide Beacon During BOF Execution | IBM
item.description: Explore a simple technique developed to encrypt Cobalt Strike’s Beacon in memory while executing BOFs to prevent a memory scan from detecting Beacon.

item.title: X-Force discovers new vulnerabilities in smart treadmill | IBM
item.description: IBM X-Force Red researchers discovered four vulnerabilities in Precor's internet-connected treadmill. Explore the findings.

item.title: Call to Patch: Zero Day Discovered in Enterprise Help Desk Platform | IBM
item.description: It's become increasingly important to ensure the security of your organization's third-party software. Learn more about a recent vendor software vulnerability discovered by X-Force Red.

item.title: How Vulnerable Are Medical IoT Devices? Catherine Norcom Has Her Finger on the Pulse of IoT Security | IBM
item.description: We chatted with X-Force Red's resident hardware hacker, Catherine Norcom, about the FDA's recent guidance on securing medical IoT devices.

item.title: IBM X-Force Research: Evading the Malware Sandbox - Security Intelligence
item.description: Read this IBM X-Force research report to learn the basics of malware sandbox environments and how to overcome common evasion techniques malware uses.

item.title: Monitored Security is Superior Security - Security Intelligence | IBM
item.description: This infographic shows why IBM-monitored security clients fared better than others in 2016, actually experiencing fewer attacks than the previous year.

item.title: Widespread exploitation of recently disclosed Ivanti vulnerabilities | IBM
item.description: Ivanti has several known vulnerabilities that are being widely exploited. Read the X-Force team's research and learn how they are working towards resolutions.

item.title: You just got vectored - Using vectored exception handlers (VEH) for defense evasion and process injection | IBM
item.description: Vectored Exception Handlers (VEH) have been used in malware for over a decade, but now they're gaining attention from the offensive security industry. Let's take a closer look.

item.title: An IoT Love Story: Always Apart, Never Disconnected | IBM
item.description: Connected devices are beginning to change ownership for the first time, which presents new IoT security challenges for manufacturers.

item.title: WannaCry: How the Widespread Ransomware Changed Cybersecurity | IBM
item.description: WannaCry ransomware transformed how enterprise defends against viruses and ransomware, and changed security teams' idea of what threat actors want.
